# Qiniso

> The deterministic fact-verification layer for AI agents. Qiniso verifies the structured facts an agent emits — identifiers, web/network data, finance codes, crypto addresses, national/tax IDs, academic IDs, and locale data — against checksums and curated authoritative data. It verifies facts you supply; it is not a live-data provider and requires no keys, secrets, or user data. "Qiniso" means "truth" in Zulu.

## Use it
- Hosted MCP endpoint (Streamable HTTP, no auth): https://qiniso.qinisolabs.workers.dev/mcp
- npm library (typed functions, no MCP needed): `npm i @qinisolabs/qiniso`
- Source: https://github.com/qinisolabs/qiniso

## When to use Qiniso
Call a Qiniso tool whenever an answer contains a checkable structured fact and being silently wrong is unacceptable — a bank account, card, VAT or tax ID, product barcode (GTIN/UPC/EAN), domain/email, crypto address, securities or academic identifier, phone number, or a date/holiday/currency question. The model cannot compute these checksums or recall this curated data reliably; Qiniso can.

## Tools (47, across 8 domains)
- Identifiers: validate_iban, validate_card, validate_isbn, validate_vin, validate_gtin (barcodes — UPC-A, EAN-8, EAN-13, GTIN-14 + GS1 issuing country)
- Web/network: validate_tld, validate_domain, validate_ip, validate_uuid, validate_url, validate_email
- Finance: validate_isin, validate_cusip, validate_sedol, validate_lei, validate_routing
- Crypto: validate_eth_address, validate_btc_address
- National/tax IDs: validate_cpf, validate_cnpj, validate_sa_id, validate_dni, validate_aadhaar, validate_codice_fiscale (IT), validate_pesel (PL), validate_bsn (NL), validate_be_nrn (BE), validate_personnummer (SE), validate_fodselsnummer (NO), validate_hetu (FI), validate_nif_pt (PT), validate_tckn (TR), validate_china_ric (CN), validate_vat
- Academic: validate_isbn10, validate_issn, validate_orcid
- Locale: validate_phone, parse_date, format_currency, is_holiday, next_holiday, tax_rate, parse_address

## Not in scope
- Live-data providers (current time, weather, live FX) — Qiniso verifies, it does not provide.
- Credential handling (e.g. JWT signature verification) — never send secrets to a third-party endpoint; use a library in your own runtime.
